nodes.title: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz
nodes.summary: Philosopher and mathematician associated with universal symbolic reasoning and the calculus of thought.

nodes.title: Wilhelm Ostwald
nodes.summary: Organizer of Die Bruecke, an effort to standardize and organize world knowledge.

nodes.title: Jorge Luis Borges
nodes.summary: Writer whose nonlinear literary structures are often treated as hypertext-adjacent ancestry.

nodes.title: The Garden of Forking Paths
nodes.summary: A Borges story frequently read as a literary ancestor of nonlinear textual structures.

nodes.title: Andries van Dam
nodes.summary: Computer scientist associated with HES, FRESS, and the Brown hypertext lineage.

nodes.title: Brown University
nodes.summary: Early hypertext research center associated with HES, FRESS, and Intermedia.

nodes.title: Hypertext Editing System
nodes.summary: An early usable hypertext system developed at Brown University.

nodes.title: FRESS
nodes.summary: A Brown University hypertext system in the early scholarly hypertext lineage.

nodes.title: Intermedia
nodes.summary: A scholarly hypermedia system associated with Brown University.

nodes.title: Nicholas Negroponte
nodes.summary: Media Lab figure associated with intelligent media and interactive information spaces.

nodes.title: Seymour Papert
nodes.summary: Constructionism and Logo pioneer who shaped computers as learning media.

nodes.title: Marvin Minsky
nodes.summary: AI researcher whose work contextualizes intelligent agents and cognitive modeling.

nodes.title: MIT Media Lab
nodes.summary: Research institution associated with media, agents, interactivity, and knowledge presentation.

nodes.title: Hans Peter Brondmo
nodes.summary: MIT figure associated with micons, or motion icons, as animated conceptual representations.

nodes.title: Micons
nodes.summary: Motion icons presented in Hyperland as animated conceptual representations.

nodes.title: Robert Winter
nodes.summary: Music scholar associated with interactive exploration of Beethoven's Ninth.

nodes.title: Interactive Beethoven's Ninth
nodes.summary: Interactive music system showing musical structure as explorable knowledge.

nodes.title: Robert Abel
nodes.summary: Interactive media artist associated with navigable multimedia art such as Guernica.

nodes.title: Interactive Guernica
nodes.summary: Interactive visual-art navigation work in the multimedia lineage.

nodes.title: Ivan Sutherland
nodes.summary: Sketchpad and head-mounted display pioneer who anticipated visual and spatial computing.

nodes.title: Jaron Lanier
nodes.summary: VPL Research founder who popularized virtual reality.

nodes.title: VPL Research
nodes.summary: Virtual reality company associated with Jaron Lanier.

nodes.title: Scott Fisher
nodes.summary: Virtual environment researcher associated with NASA VR demonstrations.

nodes.title: NASA
nodes.summary: Agency associated with virtual environment workstation demonstrations in the spatial knowledge branch.

nodes.title: Dirk Gently's Holistic Detective Agency
nodes.summary: Douglas Adams novel foregrounding the fundamental interconnectedness of all things before the public Web.

nodes.title: BBC Two
nodes.summary: Broadcaster of Hyperland.

nodes.title: Richard Saul Wurman
nodes.summary: TED founder and information architecture figure.

nodes.title: Harry Marks
nodes.summary: TED visual and media origin figure who participated in TED2.

nodes.title: John Sculley
nodes.summary: Apple executive who presented From Multimedia to Interactive Media and the Knowledge Navigator future.

nodes.title: Apple Knowledge Navigator
nodes.summary: Concept video imagining agent-mediated interactive knowledge navigation.

nodes.title: Bill Atkinson
nodes.summary: Creator associated with HyperCard and MacPaint, bringing accessible hypermedia authoring to personal computers.

nodes.title: Herbie Hancock
nodes.summary: Musician whose technology-inflected performance branch demonstrates creative digital media.

nodes.title: Nigel Holmes
nodes.summary: Information designer associated with visual explanation and data visualization.

nodes.title: Laurie Anderson
nodes.summary: Performance and media artist demonstrating narrative plus technology.

nodes.title: Bran Ferren
nodes.summary: Designer and technologist bridging entertainment, design, and technology.

nodes.title: Robert Cailliau
nodes.summary: Collaborator and promoter of the World Wide Web proposal.

nodes.title: CERN
nodes.summary: Birthplace of the Web proposal and early Web implementation.

nodes.title: HTML
nodes.summary: Document markup language that enabled simple Web publishing without rich semantic provenance.

nodes.title: URL/URI
nodes.summary: Addressing technology for distributed retrieval and identification on the Web.

nodes.title: HTTP
nodes.summary: Retrieval protocol that enabled the distributed Web.

nodes.title: NCSA
nodes.summary: Organization associated with Mosaic and the popularization of Web browsing.

nodes.title: Marc Andreessen
nodes.summary: Mosaic and Netscape figure who helped popularize the Web branch.

nodes.title: Mosaic
nodes.summary: Early Web browser that helped popularize Web browsing.

nodes.title: The Web as Incomplete Hypertext
nodes.summary: The Web solved publishing and linking, but not transclusion, authorship, semantic identity, provenance, or relationship governance.

nodes.title: Guide
nodes.summary: Personal-computer hypertext system associated with Peter J. Brown.

nodes.title: Storyspace
nodes.summary: Hypertext literature authoring system.

nodes.title: ZOG/KMS
nodes.summary: Frame and card based knowledge management and navigation lineage from Carnegie Mellon University.

nodes.title: Xanadu at Autodesk
nodes.summary: Commercial implementation attempt for Nelson's Xanadu vision during Autodesk investment.

nodes.title: Conflict and Disputed Identity
nodes.summary: The protocol pattern of preserving disagreement and possible identity matches rather than flattening them.

nodes.title: Git-backed Substrate
nodes.summary: Using Git history, branching, merging, authorship, and preservation as substrate mechanics.

nodes.title: Babel
nodes.summary: A future mapping and translation layer for machine-readable formats, languages, and ontologies.

nodes.title: Hyperland VR
nodes.summary: A future interface idea for spatial navigation of federated substrates.
